SEARCH
Package com.noorlandmc.NoorItems.enchantment

Class Enchantment

java.lang.Object
com.noorlandmc.NoorItems.enchantment.Enchantment
Direct Known Subclasses:
AutoSmelt, Hardened, Lifesteal, Strike
public abstract class Enchantment extends Object
Abstract base class for enchantments.

Provides common properties and methods for all custom enchantments, such as ID, name, applicable types, and level management.

Handles applying, removing, and managing enchantment levels on items.

  • Field Details

    • plugin

      protected final org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin plugin
      The plugin instance for this enchantment, used for creating NamespacedKeys and other plugin-related functionality.

    • key

      protected final org.bukkit.NamespacedKey key
      The NamespacedKey for this enchantment, used for persistent data containers.

    • id

      protected final String id
      The unique string identifier for this enchantment, used in the NamespacedKey and for display purposes. This is set in the constructor and cannot be changed afterwards to ensure consistency.

    • prefix

      protected final String prefix
      The prefix for this enchantment, used in lore and display. By default, this is generated from the ID by capitalizing the first letter, but it can be set explicitly in the constructor if needed. This is final to ensure that the display name of the enchantment remains consistent throughout its usage.

    • maxLevel

      protected final int maxLevel
      The maximum level for this enchantment. This is set in the constructor and cannot be changed afterwards to ensure that the enchantment's behavior remains consistent. Enchantments with higher max levels can provide stronger effects, but it's up to the implementation of the enchantment to define how the level affects its behavior.

    • applicableTypes

      protected final List<EnchantableTypes> applicableTypes
      A list of item types that this enchantment can be applied to. This should be populated in the constructor of each specific enchantment subclass to define which items are compatible with the enchantment. This is final to ensure that the applicable types for the enchantment remain consistent and cannot be modified at runtime, which could lead to unexpected behavior.

  • Constructor Details

    • Enchantment

      protected Enchantment(org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin plugin, String id, List<EnchantableTypes> applicableTypes)
      Creates a new Enchantment instance with the given plugin, ID, and applicable types. The prefix is generated from the ID and the max level is set to 5 by default. This constructor allows for quick creation of enchantments with standard settings.
      Parameters:
      plugin - The JavaPlugin instance for this enchantment
      id - A unique string identifier for this enchantment, used in the NamespacedKey and for display purposes
      applicableTypes - A list of item types that this enchantment can be applied to, defining the compatibility of the enchantment with different items

    • Enchantment

      protected Enchantment(org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin plugin, String id, String prefix, List<EnchantableTypes> applicableTypes)
      Creates a new Enchantment instance with the given plugin, ID, prefix, and applicable types. The prefix is generated from the ID and the max level is set to 5 by default. This constructor allows for quick creation of enchantments with standard settings.
      Parameters:
      plugin - The JavaPlugin instance for this enchantment
      id - A unique string identifier for this enchantment, used in the NamespacedKey and for display purposes
      prefix - The prefix for this enchantment, used in lore and display. This allows for custom display names that differ from the ID, providing more flexibility in how the enchantment is presented to players.
      applicableTypes - A list of item types that this enchantment can be applied to, defining the compatibility of the enchantment with different items

    • Enchantment

      protected Enchantment(org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin plugin, String id, int maxLevel, List<EnchantableTypes> applicableTypes)
      Creates a new Enchantment instance with the given plugin, ID, max level, and applicable types. The prefix is generated from the ID and the max level is set to 5 by default. This constructor allows for quick creation of enchantments with standard settings.
      Parameters:
      plugin - The JavaPlugin instance for this enchantment
      id - A unique string identifier for this enchantment, used in the NamespacedKey and for display purposes
      maxLevel - The maximum level this enchantment can be applied to
      applicableTypes - A list of item types that this enchantment can be applied to, defining the compatibility of the enchantment with different items

    • Enchantment

      protected Enchantment(org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin plugin, String id, String prefix, int maxLevel, List<EnchantableTypes> applicableTypes)
      Creates a new Enchantment instance with the given plugin, ID, prefix, max level, and applicable types. The prefix is generated from the ID and the max level is set to 5 by default. This constructor allows for quick creation of enchantments with standard settings.
      Parameters:
      plugin - The JavaPlugin instance for this enchantment
      id - A unique string identifier for this enchantment, used in the NamespacedKey and for display purposes
      prefix - The prefix for this enchantment, used in lore and display. This allows for custom display names that differ from the ID, providing more flexibility in how the enchantment is presented to players.
      maxLevel - The maximum level this enchantment can be applied to
      applicableTypes - A list of item types that this enchantment can be applied to, defining the compatibility of the enchantment with different items

  • Method Details

    • getId

      public String getId()
      Gets the unique string identifier for this enchantment, which is used in the NamespacedKey and for display purposes. This ID is set in the constructor and cannot be changed afterwards to ensure consistency in how the enchantment is referenced and displayed throughout the plugin.
      Returns:
      The unique string identifier for this enchantment

    • getPrefix

      public String getPrefix()
      Gets the prefix for this enchantment, which is used in lore and display. This prefix is generated from the ID by capitalizing the first letter, but it can be set explicitly in the constructor if needed. This is final to ensure that the display name of the enchantment remains consistent throughout its usage.
      Returns:
      The prefix for this enchantment, used in lore and display

    • canApply

      public void canApply(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item, int level)
      Checks if this enchantment can be applied to the given item with the specified level.
      Parameters:
      item - The ItemStack to check for enchantment applicability
      level - The level of the enchantment to check for applicability.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the enchantment cannot be applied to the item with the given level, with a message indicating the reason why the enchantment cannot be applied.

    • apply

      public void apply(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item, int level)
      Applies this enchantment to the given item with the specified level.
      Parameters:
      item - The ItemStack to apply the enchantment to
      level - The level of the enchantment to apply

    • safeApply

      public void safeApply(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item, int level)
      Safely applies this enchantment to the given item with the specified level.
      Parameters:
      item - The ItemStack to apply the enchantment to
      level - The level of the enchantment to apply
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the enchantment cannot be applied to the item with the given level, with a message indicating the reason why the enchantment cannot be applied.

    • remove

      public void remove(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item)
      Removes this enchantment from the given item, if it is present.
      Parameters:
      item - The ItemStack to remove the enchantment from

    • getLevel

      public int getLevel(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item)
      Gets the level of this enchantment on the given item.
      Parameters:
      item - The ItemStack to check for the enchantment level
      Returns:
      The level of this enchantment on the given item, or 0 if the enchantment is not present

    • getMaxLevel

      public int getMaxLevel()
      Gets the maximum level for this enchantment.
      Returns:
      The maximum level for this enchantment

    • isPresent

      public boolean isPresent(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item)
      Checks if this enchantment is present on the given item.
      Parameters:
      item - The ItemStack to check for the presence of this enchantment
      Returns:
      Whether or not this enchantment is present on the given item

    • getLoreLine

      public net.kyori.adventure.text.Component getLoreLine(int level)
      Gets the default line of lore to display for this enchantment at the given level.
      Parameters:
      level - The level of the enchantment to generate the lore line for
      Returns:
      The lore line for the given level

    • applyLore

      protected void applyLore(org.bukkit.inventory.meta.ItemMeta meta, org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item)
      Applies the appropriate lore to the given ItemMeta based on the presence and level of this enchantment on the item. This method removes any existing lore lines for this enchantment and adds the correct lore line for the current level, ensuring that the item's lore accurately reflects the enchantments it has.
      Parameters:
      meta - The ItemMeta to apply the lore to
      item - The ItemStack to check for the enchantment level and presence, used to determine which lore line to apply

    • toRoman

      protected String toRoman(int number)
      Helper method to convert an integer to a Roman numeral. This will be moved to NoorUtils eventually, but for now it's here since it's only used in enchantment lore and we don't want to add a dependency on NoorUtils just for this.
      Parameters:
      number - The integer to convert to a Roman numeral. This should be a positive integer, as enchantment levels are expected to be positive. The method does not currently handle zero or negative numbers, so it's the caller's responsibility to ensure that the input is valid.
      Returns:
      The Roman numeral representation of the given integer
